risk_factors: No validation of maximum string length in testVeryLongString could lead to potential memory exhaustion if extremely large strings are processed in production
Missing error handling tests for malformed filter expressions or invalid data types that could cause runtime exceptions
No performance testing for deeply nested expressions which could impact MongoDB Atlas query execution time
Test coverage lacks validation of thread safety for concurrent filter conversion operations in multi-threaded environments
positive_findings: Comprehensive test coverage for all MongoDB filter operators (EQ, NE, LT, LTE, GT, GTE, IN, NIN)
Proper handling of edge cases including null values, empty strings, and mixed data types
Well-structured test cases covering complex nested expressions with multiple logical operators
Good coverage of special characters and identifiers with dots in field names
Appropriate testing of primitive data types (long, float, boolean) and collections
Clean test structure following Spring Boot testing conventions with proper assertions
risk_summary: This is a well-written test file that significantly improves test coverage for the MongoDB Atlas filter converter. The main risks are around missing performance and error handling validation rather than security vulnerabilities. The added tests properly validate core functionality without introducing security concerns.
